title: [AWS] Cutting Infrastructure Costs by 55% — Graviton (ARM) Migration & Fixed-Cost Optimization
excerpt: ~20% from EC2 architecture (x86 → ARM/Graviton) alone, and up to 55% (about ₩2M/month) off by replacing ALB, NAT, and RDS with Nginx, a NAT instance, and an EC2 DB — real quotes and strategy.

title: [AWS] NAT Gateway How-To & Setup Guide — Private Subnet Internet & Cost Optimization
excerpt: A NAT Gateway lets Private Subnet EC2s use outbound internet (apt, external APIs) while staying secure — step-by-step creation, EIP attach, routing + a cost analysis and the NAT-instance alternative.

title: AWS Summit Seoul 2024 — A Backend Engineer's Field Notes (Generative AI · CMP · Middleware)
excerpt: AWS Summit Seoul 2024 — walking the booths and sorting the trends into three buckets: generative-AI serving (chatbots, email, copilots, image-gen), multi-cloud CMP, and API gateway / middleware. 'Ideas converge — UX and performance is where companies actually win.'

title: [New Grad] Hyundai Motor — CarCloud Role — Coding Test + 1st Interview (Rejected) Recap (2023 H1)
excerpt: Hyundai Motor 2023 H1 new-grad recruitment for CarCloud role — aptitude + AI interview (ViewInter, 12 questions) + coding test (Softeer, Java, 3 problems) + 1st interview (30-min pre-assignment + technical 1:3). Self-graded 4/6 well-answered; still rejected at the 1st round. Keyword recap: API Gateway, WebFlux, encryption.

title: [New Grad] KT DS — Coding Test + Interview (Final Offer) Recap (2021 H2)
excerpt: KT DS 2021 H2 new-grad recruitment — coding test (Programmers, 3 problems: Silver-4 stock sim, Silver-1 impl, Easy SQL) + aptitude + pre-assignment (1h, 1-page PPT) + 1-day interview (technical 4:1 + executive 2:N). Honest recap from a mechanical engineering + AI master's background with little IT experience.

title: [New Grad] LG Electronics — Coding Test + 1st/2nd Interview (Rejected) Recap (2022 H2)
excerpt: LG Electronics 2022 H2 new-grad recruitment for R&D Campus in Gasan (H&A division) — coding test (Programmers, Java, 3 problems, two perfect + one partial) + aptitude + 1st interview (master's/PhD research presentation, 6-page PPT) + 2nd interview (executive + English OPIc) → final rejection. A case where job-role misalignment drove the result.

title: Samsung Electronics Coding Test — Recurring Implementation Modules (Java)
excerpt: 7 implementation modules (Java) that show up repeatedly in Samsung-style simulation problems — 90° rotation, Periodic Boundary Condition (PBC), reverse direction, multi-condition sort, BFS, spiral coords, concurrent List remove. Plus 3 traps I fell into. Based on codetree / Baekjoon past problems.

title: Samsung Electronics Coding Test — 5 Attempts Recap (Samsung Research · Innovation Center · Device Platform)
excerpt: Samsung Electronics coding test — 5 attempts across grad school (DX · Samsung Research) and post-employment (DX · DS · Device Platform). 4-hour simulation problems based on Baekjoon/codetree, Python/Java/C++ allowed. Each attempt failed differently: output format omission, language-switch trap, BFS over-engineering. Honest retrospective.

title: [New Grad] SK AX (formerly SK C&C) — Coding Test + Interview (Final Offer) Recap (2023 H2)
excerpt: SK C&C (now SK AX) 2023 H2 new-grad recruitment — coding test (2h, 4 problems: sim, DP, sim, SQL aggregation) + SKCT in-depth (1.5h personality) + 1-day interview (group discussion + role/personality). Group discussion was actually fun and burned off the nerves before the role interview. Final offer.

title: The Central Limit Theorem — Definition, Experiments, and Uses
excerpt: As the sample size grows, the sample mean converges to a normal distribution for any population — verified with five distributions, plus why the CLT matters for estimation and confidence intervals.

title: How to Switch to the D Drive in Windows CMD (When cd Doesn't Work)
excerpt: In Windows CMD, cd can't move to another drive. Why you must type 'D:' to go from C: to the D drive — the Windows filesystem with an independent root per drive vs Linux's single root (/).

title: Fixing Linux 'command not found' (ls·vi·vim) — Restoring the PATH
excerpt: When you overwrite PATH on Linux/macOS and basic commands like ls·vi·vim stop working with 'command not found' — check with echo $PATH and restore the base paths (/bin, /usr/bin, etc.) with export PATH, plus how :$PATH preserves the existing paths.

title: Confidence Intervals — Meaning, Intuition, and a Common Misconception
excerpt: From why confidence intervals exist, to the hypothesis-testing view, to the truth behind the common misconception that 'there's a 95% probability the mean is inside the interval.'

title: What Are Degrees of Freedom — Why We Divide by n-1 to Estimate Variance
excerpt: Why does the sample variance divide by n-1 instead of n? Two views — correcting underestimation (bias) and degrees of freedom — to get at the essence of variance.

title: [AWS] EC2 SSH Error — Fixing UNPROTECTED PRIVATE KEY FILE (.pem chmod 400)
excerpt: When SSH-ing into EC2 you get 'WARNING: UNPROTECTED PRIVATE KEY FILE' — the .pem key's permissions are too open. Fix it instantly with chmod 400.

title: Getting Started with Python FastAPI — Concepts, Install & Running on PyCharm (feat. uvicorn)
excerpt: Why FastAPI (async / I/O-bound) and how it relates to Python's GIL, installing FastAPI and uvicorn with pip, the relationship between a web framework and a web application (ASGI/WSGI), and two ways to run the server with uvicorn in PyCharm plus the built-in Swagger UI.

title: Understanding Hypothesis Testing — Test Statistic, Null/Alternative, p-value, Significance Level, Critical Value
excerpt: From the intuition of hypothesis testing to the meaning of the test statistic, plus null/alternative hypotheses, p-value, significance level, and critical value — all connected through a single example.

title: KAIST Mechanical Engineering Grad Written Exam — Part 1: Set 1 (5 Problems, 2019)
excerpt: Aug 5, 2019 — KAIST Mech Eng grad school written exam: 4 hours (2+2), 5 subjects, 5 problems per set. Set 1: terminal-velocity ODE, Von-Mises stress, rigid-body Work-Energy, Bernoulli+Energy equation, hydrostatic force on curved surface. Passed written, failed interview.

title: KAIST Mechanical Engineering Grad Written Exam — Part 2: Set 2 (5 Problems, 2019)
excerpt: KAIST Mech Eng grad school written exam, Set 2 — Eigenvalue/Eigenvector (3-variable ODE system), full strength-design pipeline, rigid-body Work-Energy, T-v diagram thermo properties, Couette flow Navier-Stokes exact solution.

title: KSME Conference Review — Neural-Net PM2.5 Prediction (Gold Award, KSME Fluid Mechanics Competition, 2019)
excerpt: An undergraduate thesis from 2019 — using a neural network (ANN) to predict PM2.5 concentrations 6/12 hours ahead. 94,225 observations from weather + air-quality data, AIC·VIF variable selection, Window Learning. Plus the time-lag pitfall discovered two years later.

title: MS SQL Server Login, User & Schema Mapping (Compared with Oracle)
excerpt: For Oracle folks moving to MS SQL Server, the confusing 3-level Login·User·Schema structure compared with Oracle's 1-level (User=Schema=Login) model — including the sysadmin-locked-to-dbo problem and the per-task account separation fix.

title: [AWS] Cutting NAT Gateway Costs — Building a NAT Instance from an AMI (Part 1)
excerpt: A NAT Gateway costs $32+/month in fixed fees. To save money, I connected a Private Subnet to the internet using an EC2 NAT Instance — Part 1 uses the AWS community AMI (amzn-ami-vpc-nat).

title: [AWS] Cutting NAT Gateway Costs — Building a NAT Instance with iptables (Part 2)
excerpt: The Part 1 community AMI runs an old (EOS) OS and is hard to maintain. In Part 2 we build a NAT instance on the latest Ubuntu by configuring iptables directly (IP forwarding + MASQUERADE), and persist the rules across reboots.

title: Nginx Install Guide — Ubuntu / CentOS (Specific Versions, Config Structure, Firewall)
excerpt: Install Nginx on a cloud server like AWS EC2 — version-specific installs on Ubuntu (apt) and CentOS (yum), the /etc/nginx config structure (sites-available/enabled), and firewalls (UFW, firewalld, AWS security groups) all in one.

title: Fixing the DPI-1047 Python–Oracle Error on AWS Graviton (ARM) — python-oracledb Thin Mode
excerpt: Why a Python–Oracle integration that worked on x86_64 fails with DPI-1047 (can't find libclntsh.so) on AWS Graviton (ARM), and the fix using python-oracledb Thin mode — pure Python, no Instant Client (SQLAlchemy and native examples).

title: Installing Python on Windows + Setting the PATH — When 'python' Is Not Recognized
excerpt: Install Python on Windows and register the install path and the Scripts (pip) path in the system PATH so that just typing 'python' or 'pip' works in cmd. Covers the difference between user and system variables and the site-packages structure of pip-installed libraries.

title: Designing a Distributed Unique ID Generator — Twitter Snowflake (Java Implementation)
excerpt: A 64-bit unique ID generator that doesn't collide even when many servers generate IDs at once — the design intent of Twitter Snowflake (Sign·TimeStamp·DataCenter·Server·Serial, 5 segments), a Java bit-operation implementation, and a 10,000 TPS test architecture (Nginx·Netty·MongoDB).

title: Implementing t-Tests in Python with SciPy — One-Sample, Two-Sample (Student's·Welch's), Paired
excerpt: Turning the t-test theory from the previous post into SciPy code — ttest_1samp, ttest_ind (equal_var toggle), ttest_rel, with axis options. Verifying the hand-computed t and p match SciPy.

title: Testing Means — the t-test (One / Two: Student's·Welch's / Paired)
excerpt: Is a difference in two group means a real difference or just variability — the rationale for the t-test, its assumptions (normality, equal variance), and worked calculations for each type.

title: Uncertainty, Variability & Variance — Understanding the Nature of Probability
excerpt: Connecting the nature of probability through three concepts — uncertainty, variability, and variance — including the intuition for why variance is defined as 'distance from the mean.'

title: Measuring Web Performance — TTFB, Page Weight, Image Audit with curl, sips, and Python
excerpt: You don't need Lighthouse for diagnosis. curl -w timing variables give you TTFB and total time, sips reads image dimensions and bytes, and Python aggregates per-page asset weight. A 30-line script you can drop into CI for quantitative regression checks.
